title: Planes in Three Dimensions
summary: Equations of planes, distances, angles between planes, and intersection of planes and lines.
content_html:
Equation of a Plane

A plane through point P₀ = (x₀,y₀,z₀) with normal vector n = (A,B,C):

A(x-x₀) + B(y-y₀) + C(z-z₀) = 0

Expanding: Ax + By + Cz = D  where D = Ax₀ + By₀ + Cz₀

Example

Plane through (1,2,3) with normal n=(2,-1,4):
2(x-1) - (y-2) + 4(z-3) = 0
2x - y + 4z = 12

Plane Through Three Points

Given points P₁, P₂, P₃ on the plane:

v₁ = P₂ - P₁,  v₂ = P₃ - P₁
n = v₁ × v₂  (normal to the plane)
Then use point-normal form with any of P₁, P₂, P₃.

Distance from Origin to Plane

For plane Ax + By + Cz = D:
distance from origin = |D| / √(A²+B²+C²)

Distance from Point (x₁,y₁,z₁) to Plane Ax+By+Cz=D

distance = |Ax₁ + By₁ + Cz₁ - D| / √(A²+B²+C²)

Angle Between Two Planes

Planes with normals n₁ and n₂ make dihedral angle θ:

cos(θ) = |n₁·n₂| / (|n₁||n₂|)

Intersection of Two Planes

Two non-parallel planes intersect in a line. The direction of this line is n₁ × n₂.

Intersection of a Line and a Plane

Line: r(t) = P₀ + td. Plane: Ax+By+Cz = D. Substitute r(t) into the plane equation:

A(x₀+td₁) + B(y₀+td₂) + C(z₀+td₃) = D
t = (D - A·x₀ - B·y₀ - C·z₀) / (A·d₁ + B·d₂ + C·d₃)

If denominator = 0: line is parallel to plane (no intersection or line lies in plane).

Parametric Form of a Plane

r(s,t) = P₀ + s·v₁ + t·v₂
where v₁ and v₂ are two non-parallel vectors in the plane.

exercises.title: Normal vector role
exercises.prompt: The normal vector n to a plane is:
options: Perpendicular to every vector in the plane
exercises.answer: Perpendicular to every vector in the plane

exercises.prompt: Distance from origin to plane 2x + 3y - 6z = 14 is:

exercises.title: Three-point plane
exercises.prompt: To find a plane through 3 points P₁,P₂,P₃, we compute the normal as:

exercises.title: Parallel planes condition
options: They have the same D constant

exercises.title: Line-plane intersection
exercises.prompt: A line is parallel to a plane (no intersection) when:
options: The direction vector is perpendicular to the normal (d·n = 0)
The line passes through the origin
exercises.answer: The direction vector is perpendicular to the normal (d·n = 0)

exercises.title: Angle between planes
exercises.prompt: The dihedral angle between two planes is determined by:
options: The angle between their normal vectors
The angle between any two vectors in each plane
The sum of their D constants
exercises.answer: The angle between their normal vectors
